_template_meta.name: learning-cycle
_template_meta.description: Two-phase learning cycle: learning-analyst inspects recent execution data and writes evidence-backed Recommendation rows; a review step confirms the analysis. Does NOT apply changes — proposals are routed through the existing escalation / system-maintainer flow.

lookback_executions.description: Number of recent executions the analyst should inspect

task_summary: Run learning cycle: analyst inspects recent executions and proposes improvements as Recommendation rows

steps.task_description: Read the last 20 step_results, gate_results, retrospectives, scorecards, and improvement_reports from .claude/team-context/. Identify evidence-backed patterns (>=3 data points each) and write proposed improvements as Recommendation rows via the ImprovementLoop ingestion path. Persist your structured output to .claude/team-context/learning-cycle-analysis.json with keys agent_issues, gate_issues, budget_issues, retrospective_themes, unresolved_escalations. Do NOT modify code; do NOT apply changes — your output is escalated for human review or system-maintainer action.

Read .claude/team-context/learning-cycle-analysis.json. Verify each finding cites at least 3 evidence data points (task IDs, agent names, counts). Mark findings that fail the evidence bar as 'insufficient'. Print a concise summary grouped by severity. This is a soft review — proposals proceed to the existing escalation / system-maintainer flow regardless; this step exists to flag low-quality proposals before they reach a human.
